audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra vows, valuations, dedications, and tithe text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for vow valuations by age/sex, poverty adjustment, animals, houses, fields, Jubilee calculation, redemption plus fifth, firstborn, devoted things, and tithe. Strong anti-human-worth-ranking and anti-coercion safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: When a person makes a special vow involving valuation of persons to YHWH,
your valuation for a male from twenty years old to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver, according to sanctuary shekel.
If female, your valuation shall be thirty shekels.
If from five years old to twenty years old, your valuation for male shall be twenty shekels, and for female ten shekels.
If from one month old to five years old, your valuation for male shall be five shekels of silver, and for female your valuation three shekels of silver.
If from sixty years old and upward, if male your valuation shall be fifteen shekels, and for female ten shekels.
But if he is too poor for your valuation, he shall be stood before priest, and priest shall value him; according to what hand of one vowing can reach, priest shall value him.
If it is beast from which they bring near offering to YHWH, all that one gives from it to YHWH shall be holy.
He shall not replace it nor exchange it, good for bad or bad for good; and if exchanging, he exchanges beast for beast, both it and its exchange shall be holy.
If it is any unclean beast from which they do not bring near offering to YHWH, he shall stand beast before priest.
Priest shall value it, whether good or bad; according to your valuation, priest, so it shall be.
If redeeming, he redeems it, he shall add its fifth upon your valuation.
When a man sanctifies his house as holy to YHWH, priest shall value it, whether good or bad; as priest values it, so it shall stand.
If one who sanctified it redeems his house, he shall add fifth of silver of your valuation upon it, and it shall be his.
If from field of his possession a man sanctifies to YHWH, your valuation shall be according to its seed: seed of homer of barley at fifty shekels of silver.
If from year of Jubilee he sanctifies his field, according to your valuation it shall stand.
But if after Jubilee he sanctifies his field, priest shall calculate silver for him according to years remaining until year of Jubilee, and it shall be reduced from your valuation.
If redeeming, one who sanctified field redeems it, he shall add fifth of silver of your valuation upon it, and it shall stand to him.
But if he does not redeem field, or if he sold field to another man, it shall not be redeemed again.
The field, when it goes out in Jubilee, shall be holy to YHWH as devoted field; its possession shall belong to priest.
If field of his purchase, which is not from field of his possession, he sanctifies to YHWH,
priest shall calculate for him amount of your valuation until year of Jubilee, and he shall give your valuation on that day as holy to YHWH.
In year of Jubilee field shall return to one from whom he bought it, to one whose possession of land it was.
Every valuation of yours shall be according to sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
Only firstborn among beasts, which is firstborn to YHWH, no man shall sanctify it; whether ox or sheep, it belongs to YHWH.
If among unclean beasts, he may redeem according to your valuation and add its fifth upon it; if it is not redeemed, it shall be sold according to your valuation.

Only any devoted thing that a man devotes to YHWH from all that is his, from human or beast or field of his possession, shall not be sold and shall not be redeemed; every devoted thing is Holy of Holies to YHWH.
Any devoted human who is devoted from humankind shall not be redeemed; surely he shall be put to death.
Every tithe of land, from seed of land, from fruit of tree, belongs to YHWH; holy it is to YHWH.
If redeeming, a man redeems from his tithe, he shall add its fifth upon it.
Every tithe of herd and flock, all that passes under staff, the tenth shall be holy to YHWH.
He shall not inspect whether good or bad and shall not exchange it; if exchanging, he exchanges it, both it and its exchange shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
These are commands that YHWH commanded Moses for children of Israel on Mount Sinai.

translation_notes: Human valuations in this chapter are vow-payment schedules, not statements of intrinsic human worth, spiritual value, intelligence, dignity, or Divine love.
Verse 8 is crucial: poverty adjusts the valuation according to what the person's hand can reach.
House and field valuations remain tied to Jubilee timing, preventing dedication from erasing the land-return structure of chapter 25.
Redemption commonly adds a fifth, preserving the reparation/redemption pattern used elsewhere in Leviticus.
Verse 29 is severe devoted-person death material and requires deep lexical, legal, and reception-history audit; it must never authorize modern killing, coercive vows, or human sacrifice.
Tithe belongs to YHWH and is treated as holy, while exchange manipulation is blocked.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 27 to assign monetary worth to people, rank genders or ages, coerce vows, exploit poverty, justify human sacrifice, or enforce lethal devoted-person rules today. The chapter regulates ancient vow obligations and sacred property; human dignity is not priced by its valuation tables.
